narration: Did you know Earth is closest to the Sun in January?
visual_description: Hook visual: Did you know Earth is closest to the Sun in January?
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: Did you know Earth is closest to the Sun in January?
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: Did you know Earth is closest to the Sun in January?

narration: So why are we freezing in winter?
visual_description: Context visual: So why are we freezing in winter?
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: So why are we freezing in winter?
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: So why are we freezing in winter?

narration: It's all about axial tilt—not distance. Earth's axis is tilted at 23.5 degrees.
visual_description: Escalation visual: It's all about axial tilt—not distance. Earth's axis is tilted at 23.5 degrees.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: It's all about axial tilt—not distance. Earth's axis is tilted at 23.5 degrees.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: It's all about axial tilt—not distance. Earth's axis is tilted at 23.5 degrees.

narration: This tilt causes different parts of Earth to receive varying sunlight throughout the year.
visual_description: Evidence visual: This tilt causes different parts of Earth to receive varying sunlight throughout the year.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: This tilt causes different parts of Earth to receive varying sunlight throughout the year.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: This tilt causes different parts of Earth to receive varying sunlight throughout the year.

narration: In summer, the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, heating things up!
visual_description: Payoff visual: In summer, the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, heating things up!
ai_image_prompt: Payoff visual: In summer, the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, heating things up!
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Payoff visual: In summer, the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, heating things up!

narration: So, the next time you enjoy the sun, remember—it’s not about how close we are.
